architecture.summary: This is a survey paper on Sparse Autoencoders (SAEs) as a method for mechanistic interpretability of large language models. SAEs decompose model activations into a sparse set of learned features by training an encoder-decoder pair that reconstructs hidden states with sparsity constraints, enabling analysis of which learned features activate for specific inputs and model behaviors.

biblio.title: A Survey on Sparse Autoencoders: Interpreting the Internal Mechanisms of Large Language Models
